texts.full_text:
instead of using a page-based system, base your URLs on a blog post ID number to start the page, so that /blog/start/15 shows your blog starting with posting number 15. If you go this route (pun intended), you automatically get a permanent URL (so that /blog/start/15 is always going to start with the same blog entry), and you can arrange things so that you "usually" page to predictable starting numbers to facilitate caching and so on. But, if you really want to trick yesod-sitemap into generating routes with query parameters, the following standalone example may help. Here, getSitemapR is a reimplementation of Yesod.Sitemap.sitemapList that uses getUrlRenderParams in place of getUrlRender which allows processing of query parameters. I don't really know anything about conduits, so I don't know if my implementation of getSitemapR is particularly smart -- I just copied and massaged code from yesod-sitemap until it type-checked. {-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-} {-# LANGUAGE QuasiQuotes #-} {-# LANGUAGE TemplateHaskell #-} {-# LANGUAGE TypeFamilies #-} import Yesod import Yesod.Sitemap import Data.Text (Text) -- stuff needed for getSitemapR import Text.XML.Stream.Render (renderBuilder) import Data.Conduit (($=), yield, Flush(..)) import qualified Data.Conduit.List as CL import Data.Default (def) data Blog = Blog mkYesod "Blog" [parseRoutes| /blog BlogR GET /sitemap SitemapR GET |] instance Yesod Blog getBlogR :: Handler Html getBlogR = do page lookup "page" . reqGetParams <$> getRequest defaultLayout $ case page of Nothing -> [whamlet|

Top of blog|] Just n -> [whamlet|

Page #{n} of blog|] -- |Sitemap route is app route plus query parameters data SMRoute = SMRoute (Route Blog) [(Text, Text)] sitemapRoutes :: [SitemapUrl SMRoute] sitemapRoutes = map (\u -> SitemapUrl Nothing Nothing Nothing) [ SMRoute BlogR [] , SMRoute BlogR [("page", "1")] , SMRoute BlogR [("page", "2")] , SMRoute BlogR [("page", "3")] ] getSitemapR :: Handler TypedContent getSitemapR = do let = yield sitemapRoutes renderParams getUrlRenderParams let render (SMRoute r qs) = renderParams r qs respondSource typeXml $ do yield Flush urls $= sitemapConduit render $= renderBuilder def $=

texts.full_text: e hero, two special and three regular characters quite early on. In those games, the Battle Droids and Stormtroopers were our Guards and Wookiees were our specials and the Jedi Knights were our heroes. It was exclusively a Star Wars game, and it was our Star Wars game! It occurred to me in early 2014 that I could publish the rules to our Star Wars game in Kindle format, as I had previously with a I'd written for National Novel Writing Month. When I suggested to Daniel that we could sell our rules, he initially got upset because he thought that would mean we wouldn't be able to play them ourselves. After I explained to him what I actually meant - and in fairness it can't have been easy for his brain to process - he started to come round to the idea and I think I finally convinced him when I said we could give the money we made from it to charity. Naturally, I offered him the chance to choose a charity and he said "children with cancer". So I googled that and CLIC Sargent came out top off the list. I wrote the version of the Alien Worlds rulebook and published it to Amazon in June 2014. By the end of March this year (tying in with the end off the UK tax year) Alien Worlds had netted a modest £13.29 (about US$20). texts.full_text: :education now when i want create a new promotions get this error NoMethodError in PromotionsController#create undefined method `promotions' for nil:NilClass this is the controller: def create @user = User.find_by_id(params[:user_id]) @promotion =@user.promotions.create(:params[:promotion]) redirect_to promotion_patch(@promotion) respond_to do |format| if @promotion.save format.html { redirect_to @promotion, notice: 'Promotion was successfully created.' } format.json { render json: @promotion, status: :created, location: @promotion } else format.html { render action: "new" } format.json { render json: @promotion.errors, status: :unprocessable_entity } end end end help please :) A: It looks as though params[:user_id] did not contain a valid user id. Since you used find_by_id instead of find, it quietly assigned nil to @user, and of course nil doesn't have a method named #promotions, so that line failed. You need to either check for @user being nil, or change User.find_by_id to User.find and then rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound. In either case, respond with a custom 404 or whatever other way seems appropriate. One other question, is it your intention that a user can create promotions for any other user? If they should only be creating promotions for themselves, you can avoid this whole mess by just eliminating the whole User.find_by_id line, and changing the next line to: @promotion = current_user.promotions.create(params[:promotion]) Devise should have already

Globalization: The Globalization Studies Minor is designed for students seeking a liberal arts education focusing on major global issues. Students will take a variety of courses focusing on the comprehensive and multidisciplinary analysis of globalization processes.Students will gain a systematic awareness of the global forces and processes that shape our lives. A Minor in Globalization Studies provides students with a solid foundation for graduate study in a variety of disciplines, those that emphasize professional training (such as public policy, social welfare, criminal justice, business, education, law, health, and planning), as well as employment in a wide variety of public, non-profit/civil society, and private sector careers. What role does religion play? Why are some of us conservative and others liberal, and how do political differences influence our sense of right or wrong? In this lesson we will explore the ways in which cultural background can affect one's perception on morality. We'll also look at what impact religious beliefs have upon moral thought. We begin by examining some examples that illustrate these effects, and then discuss how they are related to each other. The first example is a study conducted with children from India (where there was no strong emphasis placed in the home or school about honesty) compared against those raised within an American context. The results showed significant differences between them. texts.full_text: The Birch Beat is a lifestyle blog focusing on food, wine and travel, and related lifestyle info. Gina Birch is a freelance writer and radio/TV personality in Southwest Florida Wednesday, October 20, 2010 Bye Bye Bitch I was so disappointed to learn this week that one of my favorite fun winemakers is out of business, Grateful Palate Airwines. The company not only produced good affordable wine but it was some of the most cleverly packaged. The Boarding Pass Shiraz had a label that looked like an actual boarding pass complete with a luggage tag around the neck and directions on how to enjoy the ride (or consumption) on the back. Perhaps the bottle that got the most attention was the Bitch Grenache. The word Bitch was in lovely, black script writing across a light pink label. Appropriately enough it came in a screw cap. The wine was a bit spicy with fruit on the finish…the “Bitch” was tasty. But she’s gone. I know there are bottlles out there somewhere. texts.full_text: Vision: A Visual History of Modern Christianity, based on the 2012 Cadbury Lectures delivered at the University of Birmingham, UK, appeared in 2015 from the University of California Press. Previous books include The Embodied Eye: Religious Visual Culture and the Social Life of Feeling (California, 2012), The Lure of Images: A History of Religions from Buddha to Beuys (Chicago, 2009) as well as numerous articles in journals such as Art Bulletin, Journal of Religion, Representations and Critical Inquiry. Morgan's research interests encompass a wide range of topics including the history of religious imagery; visual culture studies with an emphasis on Christianity but also Buddhism, Hinduism among others; media theory particularly digital technologies within contemporary art practices since 1980’s till day usage in diverse fields such as film making or gaming industry etc., intersections between religion and politics throughout different historical periods globally. His work has been recognized by several awards including the College Arts Association's Distinguished Teaching of Art History Award (2015), Guggenheim Fellowship for Humanities, Social Sciences & Arts among others demonstrating his significant impact in academia as well beyond it through various forms such lecture series or talks conducted at different institutions worldwide. In addition to teaching and research activities which involve supervising graduate students' dissertations related topics within aforementioned fields mentioned above; he also actively participates globally recognized conferences

texts.full_text: "tan", "ate", "nat", "bat"], Output: [ ["ate","eat","tea"], ["nat","tan"], ["bat"] ] Note: All inputs will be in lowercase. The order of your output does not matter. ``` ## 前置知识 - 哈希表 - 排序 ## 公司 - 阿里 - 腾讯 - 百度 - 字节 ## 思路 一个简单的解法就是遍历数组，然后对每一项都进行排序，然后将其添加到 hashTable 中，最后输出 hashTable 中保存的值即可。 这种做法空间复杂度 O(n)， 假设排序算法用的快排，那么时间复杂度为 O(n \* klogk), n 为数组长度，k 为字符串的平均长度 代码： ```js var groupAnagrams = function (strs) { const hashTable = {}; function sort(str) { return str.split("").sort().join(""); } // 这个方法需要排序，因此不是很优，但是很直观，容易想到 for ```js for (let str of strs) { const sortedStr = sort(str); if (hashTable[sortedStr]) { hashTable[sortedStr].push(str); } else { hashTable[sortedStr] = [str]; } } return Object.values(hashTable); }; // 测试代码 console.log(groupAnagrams(["eat", "tea", "tan", "ate", "nat", "bat"])); // 输出: [["eat", "tea", "ate"], ["tan", "nat"], ["bat"]] ``` ### 优化思路 上述方法已经足够高效，但在某些情况下，我们可以通过避免排序来进一步优化性能。一个方法是使用一个固定大小的计数数组来记录每个字符串中每个字符的出现次数，然后使用这个计数数组作为哈希表的键。这种方法的时间复杂度为 O(n * k)，其中 n 是数组的长度，k 是字符串的平均长度，因为每个字符串的每个字符都需要被访问一次。 ### 优化代码 ```js var groupAnagrams = function (strs) { const hashTable = {}; function countChars(str) { const count = new array(26).fill(0); for (let char of str) { count[char.charCodeAt(0) - 'a'.charCodeAt(0)]++; } return count.join('#'); // 使用特殊字符连接，确保不同的字符计数生成不同的键 } for (let str of strs) { const key = countChars(str); if (hashTable[key]) { hashTable[key].push(str); } else { hashTable[key] = [str]; } } return Object.values(hashTable); }; // 测试代码 console.log(groupAnagrams(["eat", "tea", "tan", "ate", "nat", "bat"])); // 输出: [["eat", "tea", "ate"], ["tan", "nat"], ["bat"]] ```

texts.full_text: 16 kHz in octave steps. Signal levels are varied from 10 to 80 dB SPL in 10 dB steps. For animals (N = 48) in the age range of 6-8 months, mean auditory thresholds were about 20 dB SPL between 2.0 and 8.0 kHz, 25 dB at 16 kHz and 30 dB at 1.0 kHz. By age 22-24 months (N = 15) thresholds had increased by about 10 dB at nearly all frequencies. By 36 months of age (N = 12), thresholds had risen further, with an average increase of 20–30 dB SPL across all tested frequencies. Notably, the most pronounced elevation occurred at 16 kHz, where thresholds exceeded 50 dB SPL in some individuals, compared to 25 dB SPL in the 6–8-month-old controls. Longitudinal data from animals followEd until 36 months revealed a progressive, nonlinear increase in thresholds, with the steepest decline occurring between 19 and 23.5 months. Cross-sectional comparisons at 36 months confirmed these trends, with older gerbils exhibiting significantly higher thresholds than age-matched controls (p < 0.01). These findings suggest that the Mongolian gerbil exhibits a hearing loss pattern consistent with presbyacusis, characterized by high-frequency selectivity and age-dependent deterioration. The absence of environmental noise exposure rules out noise-induced hearing loss as a confounding factor, supporting the model's validity for studying intrinSic aging mechanisms. Additionally, the evoked potential methodology proved reliable for tracking auditory threshold shifts

texts.full_text: and her reasons for delving into the world of Mr. Dickens! Q: Did you read any Dickens’ titles as a child? Has he been a favorite? I’m definitely a long-time fan! I don’t recall when I first began to read Dickens, but I still own some old, beat-up copies of Great Expectations, A Christmas Carol and David Copperfield that I’ve had forever. I probably read them long before I was actually ready for the material. But that’s one great thing about Dickens – you can read and enjoy his work on many levels. Q: What detail about Dickens’ life did you find most interesting in your research for the title? I’d long known that Dickens had worked in a blacking factory as a boy, but what fascinated me most in researching this book was realizing how that experience haunted him throughout his life. He never told his children about it but kept it a secret. Q: Can you speak to why the 200th anniversary of Dickens’ birth is a momentous occasion? Charles Dickens is someone who truly transcended his times, just as Darwin and Lincoln have. We still enjoy major film adaptations of his work, read or watch A Christmas Carol every Christmas Eve, and grapple with issues of urban poverty and child labor. This 2012 bicentennial celebration is a wonderful opportunity to introduce the writer to a new generation of fans, and I think A Boy Called Dickens helps to do that, especially with John Hendrix’s evocative artwork. Q: Was history your favorite subject in school? Actually, it wasn’t. Like a lot of students, I found history boring and was an English major in college. It wasn’t until I decided I wanted to write for children that I began to explore history and realize how much I loved it. To me, history is about story, and learning about the lives of p

By acceding to the Arms Trade Treaty (ATT), we aim not only at enhancing transparency within this sphere but also contributing constructively towards curbing illicit arms transfers that fuel conflicts around our world. the ATT represents an important multilateral framework dedicated to regulating the global transfer of conventional weapons in order for states parties, under strict criteria and controls as outlined by treaty stipulations. These measures include preventing diversion into illegal markets or usage against civilian populations contrary with humanitarian laws internationally recognized norms—goals which are also central within China’s foreign policy doctrine. China's accession will further demonstrate our unwavering support of multilateralism in addressing international security challenges, while reaffirming a commitment to peaceful development and cooperation. It is anticipated that the process for completing domestic legal procedures might take some time given its complexity; however specific timelines are contingent upon these proceedings' thoroughness as well. Our aim remains not only on adherence but also actively contributing towards evolving ATT practices—ultimately working together with other nations in a collective effort to forge more stable and secure environments globally. We will continue providing updates regarding our progress within this accession process, while welcoming constructive dialogue from international partners about effective implementation of the treaty's objectives. In summing up China’s decision: it underscores an earnest desire for participating as responsible actors on global security issues by adopting comprehensive legal frameworks that ensure accountability in arms trade practices and contribute toward sustainable peace initiatives. We welcome further discussions with all parties involved, aiming to make this initiative a success through cooperation based upon mutual respect—key principles which underpin our diplomatic relations globally. A UCL astronomer spoke about the importance of science experiments being carried out at schools such as measuring planetary surface temperatures using infrared radiation – one of the techniques used to observe Venus from space. Dr Green said: “The students also learned that if you are ever stranded on Mars, your best bet is probably finding a cave and waiting for rescue! If we do find life in our solar system it will most likely be microbial rather than an intelligent alien civilisation.” UCL context MSSL carries out research into the fundamental science of space physics at local scales to explore how stars are born. The laboratory also undertakes research into planetary, astrophysical and nuclear physics experiments on board satellites or probes orbiting Earth and other planets such as Venus. MSSL has been involved in over 50 spacecraft missions, including leading roles on the UK's XMM-Newton satellite for studying black holes and the European Space Agency’s Mars Express orbiter currently probing the surface of Mars with radar. In order to continue to understand the natural history of chronic migraine, address the unmet medical need, and develop effective thera...peutic strategies for this population, it is essential to establish a universally accepted classification system that can be used across different clinical settings and research studies. In recent years, there have been several attempts to standardize the diagnosis of chronic migraine. The International Classification of Headache Disorders (ICHD) has played a crucial role in shaping our understanding of chronic migraine by providing a framework for its definition and diagnostic criteria. The ICHD-3 classification system introduced two new subtypes: episodic and chronic, which are characterized by their frequency and duration. However, the exact boundaries between these categories remain unclear, leading to ongoing debate among clinicians and researchers about how best to define them. One of the main challenges in establishing a universally accepted criteria for chronic migraine is the lack of consensus on its diagnostic features. Some studies have suggested that patients with frequent or prolonged headache days may be at higher risk of developing chronic migraine, while others argue that other factors such as sleep disturbances and depression should also be taken into account. The market has been featured extensively across media outlets and continues as an iconic destination that attracts tourists while also being loved dearly for a casual outing or date night among locals. texts.full_text: The Last Of Us Is The PlayStation 3's "Fastest Selling New Software Title" The Last of Us is doing well for for Sony, quickly setting records as the fastest selling game on the PlayStation 3 for 2013, and fastest selling new software title for the console. Naughty Dog's "grim masterpiece" released on June 14 exclusi...vely on the PlayStation 3, and it has quickly become one of the most successful and critically acclaimed games in the platform's history. The game's dark, compelling narrative, stunning visuals, and emotional depth have resonated with players worldwide, solidifying its place as a standout title in the PlayStation 3 library. Since its release on June 14, 2013, has shattered sales records, becoming the fastest-selling new software title on the PlayStation 3. The game sold an impressive 3.3 million copies in its first month, a remarkable achievement that speaks to its widespread appeal. The title has since gone on to sell over 20 million copies across all platforms, including its 2020 release on the PlayStation 4 as part of the . The game's success is a testament to the skill of its developers at Naughty Dog, who crafted a story that explores themes of survival, love, and the human condition in a post-apocalyptic world. texts.full_text: the 100 samples tested, 45 were positive for Cryptosporidium by microscopy, and 52 by PCR. The prevalence of Cryptosporidium infection in diarrheic calves was 45% (95% confidence interval: 35.6-54.8) based on microscopy results, and 52% (95% confidence interval: 41.7-62.3) based on PCR results. The difference in prevalence between the two methods was not statistically significant (P = 0.12). Several risk factors for Cryptosporidium infection were identified, including farm size, calf age, and housing conditions. Calves from larger farms (>50 cows) had a significantly higher odds of being infected with Cryptosporidium compared to calves from smaller farms (odds ratio = 3.2, 95% confidence interval: 1.3-7.8). Additionally, younger calves (≤7 days old) were more likely to be infected than older calves (>7 days old) (odds ratio = 2.5, 95% confidence interval: 1.1-5.6). Calves housed in individual pens had a lower odds of infection compared to those housed in group pens (odds ratio = 0.4, 95% confidence interval: 0.2-0.9). These findings highlight the importance of Cryptosporidium as a cause of neonatal diarrhoea in calves in the Netherlands and suggest that certain management practices may increase the risk of infection. texts.full_text: of a Gradle-based Android project. Here are some steps you can follow for resolving this problem. 1) Clean the Project: First, try cleaning your entire android directory using "Build -> Clean Project" in IntelliJ IDEA or executing `./gradlew clean` command from terminal. 2)Check Your Modules: Ensure that there are no duplicate dependencies across different modules of yours project. Check all libraries and their respective build.gradle files. 3) Remove Duplicates: If duplicates found, remove one instance (usually the module causing this problem). You can do it by removing or disabling those from your `build.gradle` file: ``` dependencies { // ... } ``` 4)Check Your Third-party Libraries: The error may be caused due to third party libraries. Check if any of these are including META-INF/DEPENDENCIES. 5) Sync Project: Sometimes, syncing your project can resolve the issue: In IntelliJ IDEA -> `File` > -> Apply and OK. 6)Check For Overlapping Dependencies: You might have overlapping dependencies. Ensure that there are no duplicate dependency entries in any of module’s build.gradle files. If you're still facing issues, it may be helpful to provide more details about your project structure
